spec-init.goal: Select or create the active change in Codex and stop at the next artifact boundary.

spec-research.goal: Turn a request into proposal-ready constraints without changing product code.
mustContain: Do not modify product code in this step.

spec-plan.goal: Prepare the bounded execution handoff contract in Codex before Claude executes.
mustContain: Do not modify product code in this step.

spec-impl.goal: Dispatch bounded implementation to Claude Agent Teams by default, keep acceptance in Codex.
mustContain: Claude Agent Teams are the default execution worker.
Plain single-session Claude is only an explicit fallback
The first implementation action is dispatch, not local coding.
Before Claude dispatch completes, Codex may inspect context and prepare the packet, but must not edit product code or start implementation locally.
If `ccsm claude exec` cannot be invoked, or Claude execution cannot start cleanly, stop and report the workflow as blocked instead of continuing with local implementation.
Do not edit product code, create implementation patches, or start local implementation before `ccsm claude exec` has dispatched work to Claude.
If Claude dispatch is blocked or unavailable, stop and surface the blocker instead of silently implementing the change in Codex.
mustNotContain: If the execution packet requires Agent Teams, enable both

spec-review.goal: Run the final Codex acceptance gate before archive.
mustContain: Codex performs the final acceptance gate.
Do not archive without verification evidence.

spec-fast.goal: One Codex-native orchestration skill that advances a request through spec-init, spec-plan, spec-impl, and spec-review automatically with bounded rework loops, stopping at archive-ready by default.
